name: Agpl.Transf2D

documentation.description:
Transformations for translations/rotations in a 2D environment.
Doing Matrix * Vector' cause the described effects.
Doing Vector * Matrix  cause ???
X, Y, Angle, Homogeneous component.

array_types.name: Real_Array
array_types.code: type Real_Array  is array (Integer'Base range <>) of Real;

array_types.name: Real_Matrix
array_types.code:
type Real_Matrix is array (Integer'Base range <>,
                           Integer'Base range <>) of Real;

constants.name: Null_Pose
constants.code: Null_Pose : constant Pose := (X => 0.0, Y => 0.0, A => 0.0, H => 1.0);

subprograms.code: function "*" (V : in Pose; M : in Transformation) return Pose

documentation.description: Vector * Matrix.

subprograms.code: function "*" (M : in Transformation; V : in Pose) return Pose

documentation.description: Matrix * Vector.

subprograms.code: function "*" (M1, M2 : in Transformation) return Transformation

documentation.description: Matrix * Matrix.

subprograms.name: Absolute
subprograms.code: function Absolute (AB, BC : in Pose) return Pose

documentation.description: AC

subprograms.name: Compose
subprograms.code: function Compose (AB, BC : in Pose) return Pose

documentation.description: AC

subprograms.name: Decompose
subprograms.code: function Decompose (AB, AC : in Pose) return Pose

documentation.description: BC

subprograms.name: Get_Composition
subprograms.code: function Get_Composition (P1 : in Pose) return Transformation

documentation.description:
Get the transformation used by Compose
I.e. Get_Composition (AB) * BC = Compose (AB, BC)

subprograms.name: Get_Decomposition
subprograms.code: function Get_Decomposition (P1 : in Pose) return Transformation

documentation.description:
Get the transformation used by Decompose
I.e. Get_Decomposition (AB) * AC = Decompose (AB, AC)
I.e. Get_Inverse (Get_Composition (AB)) * AC = Decompose (AB, AC)

subprograms.name: Get_Rot_Trans
subprograms.code: function Get_Rot_Trans (A, Tx, Ty : in Real) return Transformation

documentation.description:
Optimization of Get_Rotation (A) * Get_Translation (Tx, Ty)
Don't forget that this means that the translation will be applied first
to the right vector, and then the translation

subprograms.name: Get_Rotation
subprograms.code: function Get_Rotation (A : in Real) return Transformation

documentation.description:
Matrix who provides given rotation.
This moves the reference frame, not the pose.

subprograms.name: Get_Scaling
subprograms.code: function Get_Scaling (Sx, Sy : Real) return Transformation

documentation.description: Matrix which provides given scaling

subprograms.name: Get_Trans_Rot
subprograms.code: function Get_Trans_Rot (Tx, Ty, A : in Real) return Transformation

documentation.description: Optimization of Get_Translation (Tx, Ty) * Get_Rotation (A)

subprograms.name: Get_Transf_AB
subprograms.code: function Get_Transf_AB (AB : in Pose) return Transformation

documentation.description:
Get the transformation used by Compose
I.e. Get_Composition (AB) * BC = Compose (AB, BC)

subprograms.name: Get_Transf_BA
subprograms.code: function Get_Transf_BA (AB : in Pose) return Transformation

documentation.description:
Get the transformation used by Invert
I.e Get_Transf_BA (AB) * AB = Invert_AB_BA (AB)
This is a particular case of Decompose where AC = Origin.

subprograms.name: Get_Translation
subprograms.code: function Get_Translation (TX, TY : in Real) return Transformation

documentation.description:
Matrix who provides given translation.
This moves the reference frame, not the pose.

subprograms.name: Homogeneize
subprograms.code: function Homogeneize (V : in Pose) return Pose

documentation.description: Apply the homogeneization if V (V'Last) /= 1.0

subprograms.name: Inverse
subprograms.code: function Inverse (AB : in Pose) return Pose

documentation.description:
BA
BA

Given a pose B in ref A, return A as seen from B

subprograms.name: Inverse
subprograms.code: function Inverse (X : in Transformation) return Transformation

documentation.description: Functionally, gives the inverse transformation of a given one.

subprograms.name: Invert
subprograms.code: function Invert  (AB : in Pose) return Pose

documentation.description:
BA

Given a pose B in ref A, return A as seen from B

subprograms.name: Invert_AB_BA
subprograms.code: function Invert_AB_BA (AB : in Pose) return Pose

documentation.description: BA

subprograms.name: Invert_Compose
subprograms.code: function Invert_Compose (AB, AC : in Pose) return Pose

documentation.description:
Given two poses set in the same reference frame, get the second
as seen from the first pose.
NOTE: this is equivalent to Compose (Invert (AB), AC))

subprograms.name: Relative
subprograms.code: function Relative (AB, AC : in Pose) return Pose

documentation.description: BC

subprograms.name: To_Pose
subprograms.code:
function To_Pose (X, Y, A : Real := 0.0;
                  H       : Real := 1.0) return Pose

subprograms.name: To_String
subprograms.code: function To_String (V : in Pose) return String

subprograms.name: Transf_AB_AC_BC
subprograms.code: function Transf_AB_AC_BC (AB, AC : in Pose) return Pose

documentation.description:
Given two poses set in the same reference frame, get the second
as seen from the first pose.
NOTE: this is equivalent to Compose (Invert (AB), AC))

subprograms.name: Transf_AB_BC_AC
subprograms.code: function Transf_AB_BC_AC (AB, BC : in Pose) return Pose

documentation.description:
Given a pose in some ref and a second pose in P1 ref, obtain
P2 in the global ref of P1.
